STT 2, 236 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x [...]

(o 1') [...] x [...], may it not return.

o 2'2'

[a?]-⸢a? i?-tur? ina DU₁₁.GA-ka ṣir⸣-[ti x x] x [...]

(o 2') By your exalted command, [...] ... [...], and your reliable consent, [...] cannot be revoked, let me live and be healthy, and let me make your great achievements visible, let me sing your praises.

o 3'3'

u an-⸢na?⸣-ka ⸢ki?-ni?⸣ [x] ⸢la₃ BAL⸣u ⸢lu-ub₂?⸣-luṭ ⸢lu⸣-[uš-lim-ma]

o 4'4'

nar₃-bi-⸢ka⸣ lu-[ša₂-pi da₃-li₂]-⸢li₂-ka lud⸣-[lul]

o 5'5'

⸢EN₂ an-ni⸣-[ta x x ŠID]⸢nu⸣-ma NITA₂ ana ⸢d⸣[UTU.E₃] ⸢MUNUS⸣ ana d⸢UTU⸣.[ŠU₂.A]

(o 5') He (the person affected) recites this incantation [...], and you release the male (bird) in the direction of sunrise (and) the female in the direction of sunset, and he unravels a string (made) of thyme (and) date fibre.

o 6'6'

[tu-maš-šar]-⸢ma⸣ ŠU.⸢SAR⸣ u₂[ḪAR.ḪAR ZU₂].⸢LUM⸣.[MA] ⸢i-pa⸣-[šar]

o 7'7'

⸢KU₃.BABBAR⸣ [...] NA ina SAG.⸢DU⸣-[šu₂ ...] GARan

(o 7') You put silver [...] ... on his head [...].

o 8'8'

EN₂ ⸢KU₃⸣[ku x x] ŠID?-ma? [...] ⸢SI.SA₂⸣

(o 8') He recites the incantation 'I am pure, [...]' and makes straight [...].

o 9'9'

x [...] x [x x]

(o 9') ... [...] ... [...]

o 10'10'

x [...] ⸢KI?⸣ x MA x [x]

(o 10') ... [...] ... [...]

o 11'11'

EN₂ [...] ⸢mu⸣-un-SI?-[x]

(o 11') Incantation: [...] ....

o 12'12'

inim [...] diŋir-⸢re⸣-[e-ne x ḫul]-a nam-<lu₂>-lu₇⸢lu⸣

(o 12') The command of [...] of the gods the evil [...] that approaches humanity, [...] of humanity [...], that does not know the deeds (and) [...], because the evil dough will not let [...] approach to his wisdom.

o 13'13'

niŋ₂ ⸢teŋ₃⸣ [...] ⸢nam⸣-lu₂-lu₇lu [...]

o 14'14'

niŋ₂-ak-a [...] nu-mu-un-⸢zu⸣-a

o 15'15'

niŋ₂-silaŋ-ŋa₂ ⸢ḫul-a⸣ nam-zu-a-ni-še₃ [...]  ; nu-mu-un-teŋ₃-a-gin₇

o 16'16'

a bal nam-tag-ga-a-<ni> nu-⸢mu-un⸣-teŋ₃-a-[gin₇?]

(o 16') Poured-out water, because it will not let his guilt approach.

o 17'17'

NU ŠU UB DA AL ⸢ḫul⸣-[a-ni] ⸢inim⸣ den-ki dasar-⸢lu₂⸣-[ḫi]

(o 17') It will not let the evil ... intended for him .... It is the command of Enki and Asarluhi.

Reverse
r 1r 1

⸢KA⸣.INIM.MA ḪUL ⸢BURU₅⸣.[ḪABRUD.DAmušen ...] ⸢LU₂ ŠUB?⸣ ḪUL BI

(r 1) Incantation for the evil of a rock-partridge, [...] happened [...] a man, that evil not [...] the man and his house.

r 22

ana LU₂ u E₂-šu₂ la₃ [...]

r 33

⸢KID₃.KID₃⸣-BI tam-[šil] BURU₅.⸢ḪABRUD⸣.[DAmušen x] IM ⸢DU₃uš⸣ ŠIKA SILA.LIM₂ti

(r 3) Its ritual procedures: You make a likeness of the rock-partridge [...] clay. You take a sherd from the crossroads. You place [...]. You put ... [...] on his head and in front of Šamaš you strew an offering of juniper on the [...] of the river.

r 44

⸢TIqe₂⸣ [x] ⸢GAR⸣an x x NIG₂ ⸢LUM?⸣ [x] SAG.⸢DU⸣-šu₂ ⸢GAR⸣-ma ina IGI dUTU

r 55

si-⸢ri⸣riq ⸢šim⸣LI ina [x] ⸢ID₂⸣ ta-⸢sar₂⸣-raq ⸢LU₂⸣ [x]

(r 5) You make [...] man recite the incantation 3 times in front of Šamaš. He sinks 3 times in the water and you anoint him with cypress oil (and) ... oil on his body.

r 66

⸢ina IGI⸣ dUTU EN₂ 3-šu₂ tu-⸢šad-bab-šu⸣ 3-šu₂ ina A-MEŠ ⸢i?-ṭe?⸣-[bu?]-⸢ma?⸣

r 77

⸢I₃⸣.GIŠ gišŠUR.MIN₃ gišx ⸢ina⸣ SU-šu₂ ŠEŠ₂-su

r 88

ḪUL BI ⸢DU₈⸣

(r 8) That evil is released.

r 99

⸢NAM⸣.BUR₂.BI ⸢ḪUL BURU₅⸣.[ḪABRUD.DAmušen] ana NA [x] ⸢TE⸣e

(r 9) Apotropaic ritual for the evil of a rock-partridge [...] to approach the man.

r 1010

[x] x [...] x giš⸢GIŠIMMAR⸣ [... GI?.ŠUL?]-ḪI?

(r 10) [...] ... [...] ... date-palm [...] šalālu-reed

r 1111

[x] x ina UGU x ZU? ⸢šimLI⸣ ina KA-šu₂ [x] x

(r 11) [...] ... on top ... juniper in his mouth [...] ...

r 1212

[x] EREN ⸢BABBAR?⸣ ina GEŠTU-MIN-šu₂ 1 ⸢GIN₂ NIG₂⸣.SILA₁₁.GA₂ TI-ma

(r 12) [...] white cedar in his ears, you take 1 shekel of dough and you wipe him clean. ... [...] ... the rock-partridge [...] ... of his head, you put .... ... [...] ... you wipe him clean.

r 1313

⸢tu-kap⸣-[par]-šu₂ BURU₅.ḪABRUD.DAmušen [x] x UD SAG.DU-šu₂

r 1414

x [x] x-šu₂ ⸢KI? MAḪ⸣ GAR x [x] x x ⸢tu⸣-kap-par-šu₂

r 1515

ina ⸢SAG BURU₅.ḪABRUD⸣.DAmušen GAR ⸢tu⸣-ša₂-par₂-ra-aš₂

(r 15) You put (it) on the head of the rock-partridge. You let (the bird) fly away.

r 1616

[x x] ⸢DU₁₁⸣.GA iq-bi d[60] ⸢ip⸣-šur d60

(r 16) He speaks [...]: 'Ea said (this), Ea released (this).'

r 1717

[... ša₂-me]-⸢e⸣ tu-⸢ša₂⸣-par₂-ra-aš₂

(r 17) [...]. You let (the bird) fly to heaven.

r 1818

[...] TE-šu₂

(r 18) [...] will [...] approach him.
